zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Hedge於1566年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可易亞斯特 波爾特時，她的父親Nicholas Hedge36歲，母親Alice Burrough33歲。她於大約1588年與Reverend Richard Ravens在英國艾可迪德哈姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1625年10月31日之後在英國素福爾可華迪斯費爾德，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可華迪斯費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Hedge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a hedge or enclosure, from Middle English hegge ‘hedge, fence’. In the early Middle Ages, hedges were not merely dividers between fields, but had an important defensive function when planted around a settlement or enclosure.
English: probably also a variant of   Hatch  , with voicing of the final consonant.
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Hecke  .

variants.name: Hatch
meanings: English: habitational name from Hatch (Bedfordshire, Devon, Hampshire, Somerset, Wiltshire), Hacche in South Molton (Devon), or else a topographic name from residence near a hatch, a gate leading to a forest or sometimes a sluice-gate, from Middle English hacche ‘gate’ (Old English hæcce, Kentish Old English hec(c)). This name has been in Ireland since the 17th century, associated with County Meath and the nearby part of Louth.

variants.name: Hedges
meanings: English: variant of Hedge , from the plural or, with excrescent -s.

variants.name: Hedger
meanings: English (southeastern): occupational name from Middle English heg(g)er ‘hedger, a maker or repairer of hedges’, or perhaps in some cases a topographic name for a ‘dweller by the hedge’; compare Hedge .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Hedge was born in 1566, in East Bergholt, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Nicholas Hedge, was 36 and her mother, Alice Burrough, was 33. She married Reverend Richard Ravens about 1588, in Dedham, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. She died after 31 October 1625, in Wattisfield, Suffolk, England, and was buried in Wattisfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Hedge est née en 1566, à East Bergholt, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Nicholas Hedge, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Alice Burrough, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Reverend Richard Ravens environ 1588, à Dedham, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée après 31 octobre 1625, à Wattisfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Wattisfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.East Bergholt, Babergh district, Suffolk: East Bergholt, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Hedge 1566 in East Bergholt, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nicholas Hedge 36 und ihre Mutter Alice Burrough 33. Sie hat um 1588 in Dedham, Essex, England Reverend Richard Ravens geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb nach 31. Oktober 1625 in Wattisfield, Suffolk, England und wurde in Wattisfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lei sposato Reverend Richard Ravens circa 1588, nel Dedham, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 6 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hedgeが1566年にイギリスイギリススフォルクエアスト バーグホルトで生まれた時，父Nicholas Hedgeは36歳，母Alice Burroughは33歳であった。約1588年にイギリスエセックスデドハムでReverend Richard Ravensと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1625年10月31日以後にイギリススフォルクワッティスフィールドで死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクワッティスフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hedge(이)가 1566년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 이스트 버고트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Hedge(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Burrough(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 대략 1588년 영국 에세스 데드함에서 Reverend Richard Ravens(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1625년 10월 31일 이후 영국 수폴크 왓티스필드에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 왓티스필드에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Reverend Richard Ravens aproximadamente 1588, em Dedham, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu após 31 de outubro de 1625, em Wattisfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Wattisfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Hedge родилась в 1566 г. в местности эаст бирголт, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Nicholas Hedge было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Burrough, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Reverend Richard Ravens около 1588 г. в местности дедхам, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла после 31 октябрь 1625 г. в местности ваттисфиелд, суффолк, Англия и была похоронена в местности ваттисфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.
